methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Built Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Noah Ready-Campbell and Andrew Liang, has raised $112M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 same-sector and adjacent-stage companies, comprising 8 private firms and 1 public entity. Key comparables include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses total raised ($112M) as a proxy base ($4M normalized) and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C progression, yielding $7M. Peer-median valuation across the 9-company comp set produces $1.6B, with a range of $300M (minimum) to $10.0B (maximum). The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity within robotics—from early-stage hardware developers to later-stage deployment platforms.

Triangulating these two methods yields a range of $6M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $803M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects the disparity between capital-raised multiples and market valuations in frontier robotics. Key uncertainties include built product maturity relative to peers, path to unit economics, and whether peer valuations reflect true market clearing or venture enthusiasm. A valuation toward the lower-to-middle portion of the range ($400M–$900M) may better reflect the stage and capital deployed.
